```json
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Delegation invites a general opinion; evaluating whether the contribution claim (ClarifySQL as a two-stage clarification-aware system) is coherently framed is a natural part of any holistic assessment.",
    "R1.2": "The paper chains several inferential steps — ambiguity detection → clarification policy → execution accuracy gains — whose internal coherence is routinely at stake in an open-ended 'what do you think?' review.",
    "R1.3": "Specific quantitative claims are asserted (73.4% accuracy, 6.1-pt gain, 12% reduction) with no ablation detail; verifying whether stated evidence actually supports these claims is plausibly load-bearing depending on the reviewer's intent.",
    "R1.4": "The paper explicitly positions ClarifySQL against BIRD and Spider and claims a gap only AmbigSQL v3 fills; novelty assessment is a natural focus of any research opinion.",
    "R1.7": "The paper rests heavily on 'AmbigSQL v3 (Chen et al., 2021)' — a release whose existence, feature set, and citation accuracy cannot be verified from the excerpt; if the delegation is about factual credibility, this dim becomes load-bearing."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should I focus on whether the factual and citation claims are credible (e.g., whether AmbigSQL v3 and its attributed features actually exist), or on evaluating the conceptual merit and novelty of the proposed ClarifySQL approach?"
}
```

rationale.R1.1: Delegation invites a general opinion; evaluating whether the contribution claim (ClarifySQL as a two-stage clarification-aware system) is coherently framed is a natural part of any holistic assessment.
rationale.R1.2: The paper chains several inferential steps — ambiguity detection → clarification policy → execution accuracy gains — whose internal coherence is routinely at stake in an open-ended 'what do you think?' review.
rationale.R1.3: Specific quantitative claims are asserted (73.4% accuracy, 6.1-pt gain, 12% reduction) with no ablation detail; verifying whether stated evidence actually supports these claims is plausibly load-bearing depending on the reviewer's intent.
rationale.R1.4: The paper explicitly positions ClarifySQL against BIRD and Spider and claims a gap only AmbigSQL v3 fills; novelty assessment is a natural focus of any research opinion.
rationale.R1.7: The paper rests heavily on 'AmbigSQL v3 (Chen et al., 2021)' — a release whose existence, feature set, and citation accuracy cannot be verified from the excerpt; if the delegation is about factual credibility, this dim becomes load-bearing.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus on whether the factual and citation claims are credible (e.g., whether AmbigSQL v3 and its attributed features actually exist), or on evaluating the conceptual merit and novelty of the proposed ClarifySQL approach?
